summary: Tested standings UI improvement. All 6 backend tests passed. API endpoints return required fields (total_correct_predictions, exact_score_hits, one_x_two_hits for total standings; total_correct, exact_correct, 1x2_correct for weekly standings). Admin panel standings table displays Indovinati/Esatti/1X2 columns. Frontend code verified: stats row appears BELOW player name in rankings.tsx, 'Ind.' label removed, points remain primary visual element on the right.

success_rate.frontend: Code verified (unable to fully test Expo mobile login flow via Playwright)

admin_panel_standings.ui_screenshot: Admin UI verified with Playwright - columns visible

frontend_code_review.same_layout_total_weekly: Confirmed - same renderEntry function used for both tabs

test_scenarios_verified: 1. GET /api/standings/total returns total_correct_predictions, exact_score_hits, one_x_two_hits - PASS
3. Standings sorted by points then tiebreak criteria - PASS
4. No 'Ind.' abbreviation in API response - PASS
5. Admin UI contains Indovinati, Esatti, 1X2 columns - PASS
6. Admin UI standings table has all expected headers - PASS
context_for_next_testing_agent: Standings UI improvement verified. API returns correct fields for both total and weekly standings. Admin panel at /api/admin-ui shows standings with Indovinati/Esatti/1X2 columns when viewing League Control Room -> Apri Classifica. Frontend rankings.tsx has stats row below player name (not as column next to points). Note: Expo Web login via Playwright is challenging due to React Native Pressable component - JS click events don't trigger login. Admin panel uses standard HTML and works fine with Playwright.
expo_login_note: Expo Web login testing via Playwright is challenging. The 'SIGN IN' button is a React Native Pressable component that doesn't respond to standard click events. Admin panel (/api/admin-ui) uses standard HTML and was successfully tested.
